page: Microsoft Azure D32nls_v6 is a network-optimized, low-memory virtual machine belonging to the Dnlv6 server family. Powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores operating at 4.2 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by a 260 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. There is no local storage or onboard GPU included, though the instance is premium storage capable for external block storage.

Benchmark data highlights strong memory and network performance. The instance achieves a strong rating in large-block read-only memory bandwidth at 202.93 GB/sec and static web serving throughput at 46.21 GB/sec. CPU performance is solid, with a Passmark CPU score of 39,163.2 and a multi-core stress-ng score of 26,575.61. Notably, the hardware excels in small-scale AI workloads, delivering top-tier performance in text generation for a 135M parameter LLM at 385.75 tokens/sec, and strong prompt processing speeds across both 135M and 7B models due to built-in Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions support.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D32nls_v6 optimizes resource density by pairing a high-vCPU count with a reduced memory footprint. This profile minimizes overhead costs for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive RAM allocations. The instance is highly suited for network-bound applications, static web serving, high-throughput web APIs, and lightweight machine learning inference tasks that leverage CPU-based matrix acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure D32nls_v6 is a network-optimized, low-memory virtual machine within the Dnlv6 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 32 vCPUs, 16 physical cores, a 4.2 GHz clock speed, and 64.0 GB of RAM. Lacking local storage and GPUs, it relies on remote premium storage. The instance delivers strong performance in large-block memory bandwidth (202.93 GB/sec) and static web serving (46.21 GB/sec). It also achieves top-tier results in small-model LLM text generation (385.75 tokens/sec) utilizing Intel AMX instructions. This configuration provides a cost-efficient option for compute-heavy, network-intensive workloads that require high processing power without the added cost of large memory allocations, making it ideal for web serving, API gateways, and CPU-based inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32nls_v6 is a network-optimized virtual machine featuring 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and no local storage, designed for compute-heavy and network-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32nls_v6 is a network-optimized VM with 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 64 GB RAM, built for compute-heavy and network-bound workloads.
tagline: A network-optimized compute instance pairing 32 dedicated Intel vCPUs with a lean memory profile for high-throughput web and inference workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores at 4.2 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory providing a lean 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Strong network and memory performance with 46.21 GB/sec static web serving throughput
Top-tier small-model LLM text generation utilizing Intel AMX extensions
No local storage or GPU, optimized for remote premium storage integration
Best suited for network-bound applications, web serving, and CPU-based inference
